steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
Grasby et al. investigated whether perceived category similarity predicts consumers' likelihood of purchasing brand extensions.

Evidence would be a correlation between the two: when similarity is rated high, probability changes must be high; when similarity is rated low, probability changes must be low.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
C shows exactly this: a strong positive correlation between similarity ratings and probability changes.

The other choices either measure unrelated variables (A discusses overall purchase volume), introduce a confound without showing the core relationship (B attributes both to brand recognition), or show the opposite (D: ratings varied while probability changes stayed similar).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
